MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-11-2024 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-27-2023 Continued To Sep-16-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS, OR ALT FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER (ADDED FOR THE POSTING OF TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Text Ruling

Motion To Quash Subpoenas, Or Alt For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM EMERSON's Motion To Quash Subpoenas, Or Alt For Protective Order.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. In this action for wrongful termination of employment, defendant Mantle, Inc., has subpoenaed employment records from two of plaintiff Emerson's prior employers, Entegris, Inc., and Revasum, Inc. Plaintiff Emerson has moved to quash the subpoenas or for a protective order on grounds of privacy and overbreadth. Employment records sought by the subpoenas are within the zone of privacy protected under California law. Alch v. Superior Court, 165 Cal.App.4th 1412, 146-1427 (2008); Board of Trustees v. Superior Court, 119 Cal.App.3d 516, 526-527 (1981). Discovery of such documents over a privacy objection is permitted only by the countervailing demonstration of a compelling state interest. There is such an interest in "facilitating the ascertainment of truth in connection with legal proceedings". Britt v. Superior Court, 20 Cal.3d 844, 857 (1978); In re Lifschutz, 2 Cal.3d 415, 432 (1970). Defendant urges its affirmative defenses as presenting issues of proof as to which there is such a compelling state interest. Several such defenses may be informed by the sought-for employment records, particularly the defenses of after-acquired evidence and unclean hands. However, prior employment records could not bear on Emerson's job performance at Mantle, except as inadmissible evidence of character. Evidence Code sec. 1104. In any case, Defendant's access to such records must be "narrowly circumscribed" to matters of direct relevance to the issues in litigation. Britt v. Superior Court, 20 Cal.3d 844, 849 (1978) Applying these standards to the specific categories of records requested in the subpoenas, the motion to quash will be granted in part and otherwise denied. Specifically, records covering or touching the following categories shall be produced, all as to William Emerson: job applications; work activities; wages and/or salary; job titles; job duties; job descriptions. Records covering or touching following categories shall not be produced: requested accommodations; designation (or not) as an "exempt" employee; job performance; performance evaluations; performance improvement plans.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
